feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Structured Material with decentralized EWM
entry.content:
We have decentralized EWM and have some structured materials (in our case: Displays). It's not possible to do the explosion manually in S/4 (error L9526) or during Goods Receipt (error VL583). After some research I come to the conclusion that a "normal" explosion is simply not supported.

My question is: what would be the advised work-around?

I've tried some options in both S/4 and EWM and it seems to me that the only option would be to manually issue the Display and receive its components with, for instance, movement types 502 and 501. Does anyone have a better suggestion?

entry.title: ME2ON cannot be used with Account Assignment "M" in Subcontracting PO
entry.content:
Hi all,

Would like to check how could we resolve the followings?

When the subcontracting orders are account-assigned to a sales order, the components are not transferred (using a transfer posting) to the "stock of material provided to vendor" but are directly withdrawn from the sales order stock the time of the goods receipt. The material manufactured is also posted to the sales order stock. In such circumstances, a Subcontracting PO with Account Assignment Category "M" will not appear in ME2ON because no need to transfer stock to Subcontractor.

How could user generate the mandatory forms? E.g. Delivery output form that is needed for the shipment to happen? We need to trigger all these forms from delivery order. The user will not be able to do so since now Delivery Order is skipped due to there is no more usage of ME2ON. Or is there any possibility to generate the outbound Delivery Order for subcontract PO besides ME2ON? Thanks!

entry.title: Croatia e-Invoicing to Moj eRacun
entry.content:
How to connect to Moj e-Racun using S4HANA Cloud Document and Reporting Compliance?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Requirement 77 (CCS: Accr from Settl) assigned to the Condition type REA5
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In the Standard Pricing procedure for Settlement A10005 (Rebates - Germany), Requirement 77 (CCS: Accr from Settl) is assigned to the REA5 condition type. However, in the pricing procedure A17005 (Rebates US), it is not assigned to the condition type REA5. Therefore, I would like to understand the functionality or purpose of the routine 77 assigned to REA5. If it's not assigned, what are the implications?

Please advise.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: 3PL warehouse Integration with SAP
entry.content:
I am using Basic Type: SHP_OBDLV_SPLIT_DECENTRAL02 AND MESSAGE TYPE :SHP_OBDLV_SPLIT_DECENTRAL from 3PL warehouse to split the delivery in SAP S4 HANA. But the idoc is failing with Status 51 error  .

Error Detail: 

Error in document 0080000698 item 000010 (quantity consistency check).

I have filled the idoc segment:

Please help here what else need to be updated in the idoc segment.

Thanks,

Arjun

entry.title: fiori configuration with logon group
entry.content:
Bearing in mind that our S/4HANA system has Fiori in the same instance, can you tell us how we should proceed to manage limiting executions to a logon group?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Payment Term configuration not obtaining the desired result
entry.content:
I need to create a new Payment Terms that involves that payment will be made after 30 days on the 10th of the month.

For example if the Document date is 18.05.2024 calculated due date should be 10.07.2024

Document date 18.05.2024 + 30 days --> 17.06.2024--> 10.07.2024 (day 10th)

Another example if the Document date is 02.06.2024 calculated due date should be 10.07.2024 as well

Document date 02.06.2024 + 30 days --> 02.07.2024 --> 10.07.2024 (day 10th)

I have done many different configurations for the new Payment Terms but I don’t get any combination to calculate net due date 10.07.2024 in both cases when Document date is 18.05.2024 and 02.06.2024.

Do you know if there is a way to configure the Payment Terms to get the result that I need?

entry.title: Multi-Input field on RAP Based Fiori Elements Object Page
entry.content:
We are building an RAP based Fiori Elements Application and need to provide one multi-input field on the object page. SAP's standard documentation seems to be reference CAP based Fiori elements as the syntax provided gives an error when I try to implement the steps.

 

Can you please provide the documentation for adding a field as Multi-Input in the object page for RAP based Fiori elements.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Embracing SAP Clean Core: A Strategic Imperative for Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving digital landscape, enterprises are constantly seeking ways to streamline operations, enhance agility, and drive innovation. SAP Clean Core emerges as a pivotal strategy in this quest, offering a path to a simplified, efficient, and future-ready enterprise core. This blog post explores the significance of SAP Clean Core, its alignment with SAP Activate methodology, the synergies with RISE with SAP, the transformative capabilities of S/4HANA, and the role of KTern.AI in facilitating this journey.

SAP Activate Methodology: The Blueprint for Success

The SAP Activate methodology is a flexible, agile framework designed to guide businesses through the implementation and deployment of SAP solutions. It encompasses best practices, guided configurations, and a clear methodology to ensure project success. By leveraging SAP Activate, organizations can accelerate their SAP S/4HANA implementations, ensuring a clean core that is devoid of unnecessary customizations and complexity.

RISE with SAP: Elevating Business Transformation

RISE with SAP is a comprehensive offering that simplifies the migration of ERP systems to the cloud. It encapsulates cloud solutions, infrastructure, and services, providing a seamless transition path. With RISE with SAP, businesses can benefit from embedded intelligence, real-time insights, and advanced technologies, all of which contribute to maintaining a clean core and fostering business resilience.

SAP S/4HANA: The Heart of Digital Transformation

At the core of the digital transformation journey lies SAP S/4HANA, SAP’s most advanced ERP suite. Designed to run on the in-memory SAP HANA database, SAP S/4HANA integrates functions from lines of business and industry solutions, offering unparalleled speed, simplicity, and efficiency. A clean core in SAP S/4HANA ensures that businesses can leverage real-time data processing and analytics, essential for informed decision-making and competitive advantage.

 

The Imperative of SAP Clean Core

A clean core is essential for businesses to adapt swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer expectations. It enables organizations to leverage the latest SAP product advancements and integrate new software releases promptly. Without a clean core, businesses face challenges such as increased operational costs, inflexibility, and technical debt, which can hinder innovation and growth.

 

 

Without a clean core, organizations may encounter several challenges:

Technical Challenges

  • Complex Upgrades: Customizations can complicate the upgrade process, leading to longer and more costly system updates
  • Reduced System Performance: A cluttered core with custom code can degrade system performance and reliability
  • Increased Technical Debt: Over time, customizations can accumulate, resulting in technical debt that hampers agility and innovation.
  • Integration Difficulties: Custom code can create dependencies that make it difficult to integrate with other systems or technologies.

Operational Challenges

  • Operational Instability: A non-standard core may lead to unpredictable system behavior and increased downtime
  • Compliance Risks: Deviations from standard configurations can pose risks to regulatory compliance and data integrity
  • Resource Intensiveness: Customized systems require more resources for maintenance and support

Strategic Challenges

  • Inhibiting Digital Transformation: A dirty core can hinder an organization’s ability to adapt to new business models and technologies.
  • Barriers to Innovation: Excessive customizations can limit the ability to leverage new SAP functionalities and innovations.
  • Change Management Difficulties: Transitioning to a clean core requires significant change management efforts, which can be challenging if the existing system is heavily customized.

In summary, not maintaining a clean core can lead to increased costs, reduced efficiency, and a lack of flexibility, which are detrimental to an organization’s digital transformation journey. It’s essential to adopt a strategic approach to ensure a clean core for long-term success and sustainability.

Organizations looking to clean up their SAP core can start by adopting a strategic approach that includes the following steps:

  1. Assessment of Customizations: Begin with a thorough assessment of the existing system to identify all customizations, modifications, and enhancement.
  2. Fit-to-Standard Analysis: Evaluate the necessity of each customization against the standard SAP S/4HANA capabilities. Retire any custom code that is no longer needed or can be replaced by standard functionality.
  3. Leverage SAP Best Practices: Adopt SAP best practices and guided configurations to align with standard processes.
  4. Use of SAP BTP for Extensions: Utilize SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to build extensions rather than modifying the core system.
  5. API-First Approach: Implement an API-first strategy to facilitate integration without altering the core system.
  6. Continuous Monitoring and Optimization: Establish a process for ongoing monitoring and optimization to ensure the core remains clean and aligned with business needs.
  7. Documentation and Governance: Maintain comprehensive documentation of all changes and establish strong governance to manage technical debt.

 

By following these steps, organizations can systematically clean up their SAP core, leading to a more streamlined, efficient, and agile system that is better equipped for the digital transformation journey.

 Accelerating the Journey to a Clean Core with AI powered Automation

KTern.AI stands out as a digital transformation suite that aids in achieving a clean SAP core It offers digital tools for assessment, governance, process orchestration, testing, and optimization. With KTern.AI, businesses can plan risk-free SAP transformations, ensure productive governance, orchestrate SAP process lifecycles, and avoid disruptions during releases.

In conclusion, maintaining a clean core is not just a technical necessity; it is a strategic imperative that underpins the digital transformation journey. By embracing SAP Activate methodology, RISE with SAP, and SAP S/4HANA, and leveraging tools like KTern.AI, businesses can navigate the complexities of digital transformation with confidence and clarity


For more reading: 

@https://blogs.sap.com/2023/07/01/clean-core-demystified-what-does-it-mean-and-how-to-achieve-it-with... 

@ https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud... 

.

entry.title: which parameter is required to generate expensive statement trace?
entry.content:
which parameter is required to generate expensive statement trace (2 correct answers)

Trace status, 
Threshold duration, 
passport trace level
Trace file

entry.title: S/4HANA, allocation cycles - receiver basis, does not save.
entry.content:
I am setting up allocation cycles, but when i populate Receiver Basis - it will not save. 

  1. Manage allocations
  2. Pick a cycle
  3. Edit a cycle’s segment by uploading a csv with % split
    1. At this point, it accepts the spreadsheet and confirms total comes to 100% (see screenshot 1 below even saves the draft).
    2. When I click save I get … see screenshot 2 (it won’t save)

 

 

Screenshot1

 

 

 

Screenshot2 (removed, replaced with text) 

Total percentage for segment x is less than 100. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Automating Purchase Order Creation from Purchase Requisition in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: How can we automatically create a Purchase Order from a Purchase Requisition when the associated Sales Order has a net price less than a certain configurable amount in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? We are aware of the option using iFlows through BTP and APIs, but is there a better way to automate this directly in S/4HANA, given that application jobs do not provide parameters around price?

entry.title: Where is field CMPSB in S4Hana
entry.content:
Hi All

While working on ATC I got to a field that was not mapped from VBUK to VBAK. The field  is CMPSB - Dynamic check

Does anyone know where is the Dynamic check found in S/4HANA?

Thanks

Andrea

entry.title: Creation of product master for retail system via Soap service is recommended?
entry.content:
Hello,

We working for one of our client on transformation from ECC to S4 Hana Private Cloud(On-premise)

Here, I need create Article Master or Product master (Retail System) via API's.

I have checked with the ODATA V2 and ODATA V4 API's and understood that we cannot use both the services for Retail Maintenance which is written in the respective API page.

Now i am looking for SOAP service and found the following API "Overview | Product Master - Replicate from Client to SAP S/4HANA | SAP Business Accelerator Hub". 

Please any let me know whether this SOAP service can be used for Retail System or not to create article master?

 

Regards,

Sunil

entry.title: Regarding S/4HANA Web UI Kit
entry.content:
Earlier, your team provided with 2 libraries for UX designers to use

  1. S/4HANA Web UI Kit (Compact)
  2. S/4HANA Web UI Kit (Cozy)

Are the two libraries now merged into a single one which is called as S/4HANA Web UI Kit? In which case, are you planning to merge all the components from the above two into this one, as there are quite a lot of them which aren't available in the latter. I am assuming your team is currently merging this (in progress).

Secondly, there's also a separate section in the Fiori guidelines called as 'UI Elements - SAP Web Components'. When designing for teams using SAP Web Components - which Figma library does the UX designer refer to?

SAP Digital Manufacturing

entry.title: BP creation according to BP category chosen
entry.content:
Hello

I am looking for a way to have the system proposing a different selection of BP grouping based on the BP CATEGORY the user choose when creating a new business partner.

Exemple:

--> I want to create a new BP, BP CATEGORY person: the system will allow me to create within the BP grouping X,Y or Z

--> I want to create a new BP, BP CATEGORY organization: the system will allow me to create within the BP grouping A, B or C.

 

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Best Way to Reserve Stock Without MRP Impact
entry.content:
Hi all,

What is the best way to reserve stock in SAP, so that it has no MRP impact and new POs are not triggered? Is there a particular movement type that it would be advisable to use in order to achieve this please? Or is there any other way altogether that not involve reservations? The system should also prevent the reserved qty from being consumed. 

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Bunie

entry.title: How to update multiple PR line items using a standard API
entry.content:
Hello,

        We are using a standard PR API for creation of purchase requisition with multiple line items using AIS (Azure integration service). Now our issue is when we are trying use a patch call to update multiple line items of the PR (ex change of quantity of line item 10, 20, 30....), the 1st line is getting updated, but  when AIS is trying to update 2nd line item getting an error- "User XXX cloud connector, SBP already processing purchase Requisition XXXXX".

Is it not possible to update the multiple line items of a PR via single update patch call of the PR requisition.

Please let me know the possible way.

Link to standard API: https://api.sap.com/api/OP_PURCHASEREQUISITION_0001/overview

 

Regards

Venu

entry.title: Issue with address inconsistencies when synchronizing Employee to Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, I have been requested to solve an issue related to a BP in S4 on premise.

To give context: We had a BP not synchronized correctly with its employee number (from PA30).

Our functional team executed RSUID_REMOVE_FALSE_BP_ASGN to unlink them and when executing /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE to generate a new BP and link it with the employee number we received the following 2 error logs:

Message R1000 "Error_Time Dependency_Addresses ADVW_VALIDITY_ERROR"

Message R11245 "Error for validity periods of address usages"

I have checked the address' validity periods in the HR master (PA30) and the BP and they match, also I have checked BUT020, ADRC and PA0006 tables and they match too. 

Reading sap note 2578294, it suggests to review the path "Cross-Application Components=>SAP Business Partner=>Business Partner=>Basic Settings=>Address Determination" and make sure the address type XXDEFAULT is set on "Assign Transaction to Address Type", and it is. Also I have checked sap notes 2517507 and 2598468, but they cannot be implemented in the system. 

Going on, we have executed again /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE but with the flag "Override Banks, addresses and roles" and it gives us a different error log:

Message no. AM266 Personal address not deleted; other reference exists.

Searching for it I have found this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-questions/bupa-del-error/qaq-p/10741420, it says that the error generally occurs for the addresses  of table BCST_CAM, I have checked it and there isn't any entry for the employee number, could that be the problem? Going trough note 835936, I have executed RSBCS_ADDRESS_REFERENCE, but it says that there isn't any reference. 

Does someone know what else can I do? Or how should I proceed? 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: How to import LTMC Migration Projects to the Migration Cockpit Fiori app
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Our team has recently upgraded our SAP S/4HANA 2020 to SAP S/4HANA 2023, and after doing so, we noticed that the Migration Projects shown in transaction LTMC don't show up in its new corresponding Fiori app (Migrate Your Data - Migration Cockpit).

We can still see our Migration Projects in LTMC and use the new Fiori app without any issues, but we'd like to know:

  • Is there a way of importing the Migration Projects in transaction LTMC to the Migrate Your Data Fiori app?

Thanks a lot for your support!

Best regards,
Data Migration team


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
